commit.message:
Fix setup.ps1 encoding: add UTF-8 BOM for PowerShell 5.1

PowerShell 5.1 on Windows defaults to system ANSI codepage (GBK on
Chinese systems), causing Chinese characters to be garbled and parsed
as syntax errors. UTF-8 BOM makes PS 5.1 read the file correctly.

commit.message:
Add noise profiler, benchmark suite, training pipeline (#533, #536, #542) (#577)

* Fix backend container crash — create data_dir before SQLite init (#448)

The container crashes on startup because SQLite can't open the database
file at ~/.terrascry/datasets/terrascry.db — the parent directory doesn't
exist in the container filesystem. Ensure data_dir is created when
computing the default database URL.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix frontend deploy verify — use az CLI health check instead of curl (#450)

The frontend is behind Cloudflare Access, so curl gets a 403. Use
az containerapp revision list to check the revision health state instead.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add marketing landing page at terrascry.com (#452)

Astro static site at platform/landing/ deployed to Cloudflare Pages.
Dark zinc + emerald design matching the platform design system.
Sections: hero, platform triad (Pathfinder/HIRT/GeoSim), workflow,
use cases (archaeology/UXO/defense/research), ecosystem map, CTA.

DNS updated: terrascry.com now points to CF Pages instead of Azure.
CI workflow deploys on push to main via wrangler.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove hardcoded demo from WelcomeOverlay (#454)

Replace "Load Demo Survey" (hardcoded iron_age_hillfort) with a proper
empty state: "Open Mission File" + "Start Live Survey" as primary
actions, with reference datasets listed below for quick access.

No more "demo" concept in the app — users load data through File menu
or select from available reference datasets.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Show backend connection status honestly (#456)

When the backend is unreachable:
- Toolbar shows red "offline" badge instead of empty version
- WelcomeOverlay shows "Backend unavailable" with clear message
- All backend-dependent actions are hidden until connected
- No more silent failures that lead users on

When connected:
- Toolbar shows actual version from /api/runtime
- WelcomeOverlay shows action buttons and reference datasets

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix frontend deploy verify — use grep instead of exact match (#458)

The az CLI returns health status for all active revisions (one per line).
When multiple revisions are active during rollover, the exact string
match `[[ "$HEALTH" == "Healthy" ]]` fails because the output contains
multiple lines. Use `grep -q` to check that at least one is healthy.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx envsubst destroying proxy config variables (#460)

The default nginx:alpine 20-envsubst-on-templates.sh runs envsubst on
ALL $VAR patterns, replacing nginx variables ($host, $uri, $remote_addr,
$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for, etc.) with empty strings. This broke the
proxy_pass and proxy_set_header directives, causing /api/ requests to
fail with 404.

Fix: use a custom entrypoint that runs envsubst with only $BACKEND_ORIGIN,
preserving all nginx internal variables.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use NGINX_ENVSUBST_FILTER instead of custom entrypoint (#462)

The previous fix (custom entrypoint with CMD override) didn't work in
Azure Container Apps. Use the built-in NGINX_ENVSUBST_FILTER env var
to restrict envsubst to only BACKEND_ORIGIN, preserving nginx variables.
Template goes back into /etc/nginx/templates/ (the standard location).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use $proxy_host to match ACA Envoy Host routing (#465)

$host sends the frontend's domain (app.terrascry.com) as the Host header
to the backend's internal Envoy ingress. ACA Envoy routes by Host header
even on internal traffic, rejecting requests with a 1946-byte 404 before
FastAPI ever sees them. $proxy_host sends the backend's internal FQDN,
which Envoy accepts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix BACKEND_ORIGIN to use HTTPS for ACA internal traffic (#467)

Backend ingress has allowInsecure=false, so ACA Envoy returns 301
redirects for HTTP requests even on internal traffic. Frontend nginx
proxied to http:// and got 301s back — requests never reached FastAPI.

Changed all BACKEND_ORIGIN values from http:// to https:// in the
deploy workflow. ACA provides trusted internal TLS certs between
containers in the same environment.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use HTTP + allowInsecure for internal ACA traffic (#469)

The full root cause chain:
1. $host sent wrong Host header → fixed with $proxy_host (PR #465)
2. http:// BACKEND_ORIGIN got 301-redirected by Envoy (allowInsecure=false)
3. https:// BACKEND_ORIGIN failed SSL handshake — backend IP restrictions
   only allow Cloudflare IPs, rejecting internal container traffic

Fix: revert to http:// and set allowInsecure=true on backend ingress.
Internal container-to-container traffic uses HTTP on port 80. External
traffic is secured by Cloudflare IP restrictions + CF Access.

Verified live: /api/runtime, /api/edge/health, /api/reference-datasets
all return 200 OK through the frontend proxy.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix import dialog freeze when cancelling file picker (#471)

The invisible file input overlay (absolute inset-0 opacity-0) inside the
Radix Dialog's focus trap caused the page to freeze when the native file
picker was cancelled. Replaced with a hidden input triggered via ref click.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve import dialog UX — success feedback, URL import, file name (#473)

- Show green checkmark + filename on success, auto-close after 800ms
- Add URL paste field for direct download import (Enter or Load button)
- Show filename during upload spinner
- Prevent clicking drop zone while busy

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix overlay z-index — playback bar above colorbar, VE badge repositioned (#475)

- TimelineScrubber and PlaybackControl: z-10 → z-20 (renders above colorbar)
- VE badge: bottom-3 → bottom-16 (clears playback bar in split view)
- Colorbar stays at z-10 — playback controls take visual priority

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enable satellite basemap by default with semi-transparent heatmap (#477)

Survey data now loads with geographic context visible — satellite basemap
on by default (showSatellite: true) and heatmap opacity at 0.8 so the
basemap shows through. Users can still adjust via the Layers panel.

Removes the empty-state-only basemap fallback in MapView that previously
hid satellite imagery whenever data was loaded.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix deploy workflow — use HTTP for internal backend origin (#479)

Internal ACA container-to-container traffic uses HTTP (allowInsecure=true
on backend). HTTPS causes SSL handshake failures due to IP restrictions
blocking internal container IPs. This was being reset on every deploy.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move playback controls to dedicated layout footer (#481)

Replace floating absolute-positioned timeline scrubber with a docked
footer bar at the bottom of the workspace. The map/3D panels resize
to accommodate the footer, eliminating z-index conflicts with the
colorbar and other map overlays.

- TimelineScrubber: full-width footer with single-row layout
- SplitWorkspace: flex-col with content area + footer
- SceneView VE badge: revert to bottom-3 (footer handles spacing)
- PlaybackControl: revert z-index to z-10

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Mapbox token, Google tiles, and DEM/hillshade providers (#484)

- Wire VITE_MAPBOX_TOKEN through Dockerfile build arg + deploy workflow
- Add Mapbox Streets, Satellite + Labels styles
- Add Google Satellite, Hybrid, Terrain, Roads tiles
- Add ESRI Hillshade, ESRI Hillshade Dark, ESRI World Topo
- Support numeric subdomain balancing for Google tile servers

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Drape basemap on 3D terrain + Mapbox token + expanded tile providers

3D terrain mesh now composites basemap tiles underneath the heatmap,
matching the 2D view. Basemap and heatmap blend using the layer opacity
setting from the Layers panel.

- New useBasemap3DTexture hook: fetches tiles, composites into Three.js texture
- TerrainMesh: accepts basemapTexture, blends with heatmap at configurable opacity
- SceneView: passes basemap texture and heatmap opacity to terrain
- Export tile helpers from useBasemapTiles for shared use
- Wire VITE_MAPBOX_TOKEN through Dockerfile build arg + deploy workflow
- Add Google (4), ESRI Hillshade (2), Mapbox Streets/Satellite+Labels, ESRI Topo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 3D terrain lockup — memoize range prop + defer basemap loading

The range prop [rangeMin, rangeMax] created a new array every render,
causing TerrainMesh's expensive canvas compositing useMemo to fire
repeatedly. Memoize it so it only recomputes when values change.

Also defer 3D basemap tile loading until a dataset is present to avoid
competing with the 2D view for network requests on initial page load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix origin reference instability causing 3D lockup

useDefaultOrigin called JSON.parse (loadStoredOrigin) on every render,
creating a new object reference each time. This cascaded through
useBasemap3DTexture → tileSpecs recompute → new texture → re-render loop.

Fix: memoize the stored origin read. Also reuse terrainRange for DepthSlice3D.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Redesign landing page from sales pitch to engineering showcase (#489)

Pivot terrascry.com from B2B marketing funnel to authentic build-in-public
engineering showcase. Rewrite all copy to reflect open-source/open-hardware
R&D project targeting researchers, archaeologists, and recovery teams.

- Add Origin section with first-person project motivation
- Add status chips (Prototype/Design Phase/Active Development) to instruments
- Add [Target] labels to unvalidated workflow outcomes
- Reframe applications: Defense & Intelligence → Recovery & Forensic Archaeology
- Rewrite ecosystem as Software section with honest status badges
- Replace "Request Early Access" / "Request a Pilot" CTAs with "Get in Touch"
- Replace "Sign In" nav button with "Get in Touch" mailto link
- Remove Intellimetrics from footer, add license info
- Keep existing design system (dark theme, emerald accent, glass cards)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Redesign 3D view: flat terrain, honest subsurface, basemap fallback (#491)

Implement AI counsel consensus (Codex 0.97 / Gemini 0.98) to fix the 3D
subsurface visualization:

- Flatten TerrainMesh: remove gradient-to-Z vertex displacement; signal
  maps to color/opacity only, Z-axis reserved for physical depth
- Add Cartesian grid fallback when no basemap tiles available (replaces
  silent grey plane)
- Show "No georeferenced basemap" badge when tiles fail
- Gate DepthSlice3D and CrossSection3D behind validation mode with
  "Heuristic — Not Inversion Data" warning badge (synthetic exp decay
  is geophysically dishonest for operational use)
- Move camera orbit target to z=-1m so user pivots around buried
  objects, not the surface
- Lower default camera angle for better subsurface viewing
- Keep VolumeRenderer3D as the only real subsurface volume path
- Make terrain always transparent for see-through to buried objects

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enable 3D view on mobile devices (#493)

The mobile tier gate was blocking 3D scene rendering entirely (allow3D:
false). With the flat terrain redesign removing vertex displacement and
gating synthetic depth layers, the 3D scene is now lightweight enough
for mobile GPUs. Enable it so the 2D/3D toggle works on phones.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add geophysical training data acquisition pipeline (#510)

Implements a 5-stage pipeline (download → normalize → validate → features → publish)
for acquiring and processing open geophysical datasets. Downloads from PANGAEA,
Mendeley, Kaggle, USGS, Ensaio, Deep Blue, Bristol, and NOAA sources. Normalizes
to silver-layer Parquet with standardized schema. Extracts noise profiles and anomaly
statistics for physics engine parameterization. Publishes to ADLS Gen2 medallion
architecture.

28 datasets normalized, 65.3M data points, 8.7 GB on disk.

Closes #508

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add INTERMAGNET HAPI and Alaska DGGS downloaders, expand PANGAEA series (#512)

- Add INTERMAGNET observatory downloader via confirmed HAPI API (10 stations,
  minute-cadence X/Y/Z/F data for diurnal correction reference)
- Add Alaska DGGS aeromagnetic downloader (statewide magnetic map + regional surveys)
- Update NOAA observatory downloader to use working INTERMAGNET HAPI API
- Expand PANGAEA series download limit to 100 (was 20) to grab all 95 children
  from parent series 963897
- Update research doc with verified download URLs for Alaska DGGS, INTERMAGNET
  HAPI API details, and ADS access notes
- Add CLI commands: download intermagnet, download alaska-dggs

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add batch validation and feature extraction CLI commands (#514)

Adds `validate-all` and `features all` commands to the data pipeline CLI
for processing all normalized datasets in one pass. Includes skip-existing
support for incremental runs as PANGAEA normalization continues.

Batch results on 42 datasets: 27 pass validation, 14 medium/high confidence
noise profiles (floor 3-6 nT for archaeological gradiometry), 4,344 detected
anomalies across 33 datasets.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add NetCDF and compressed CSV support to generic normalizer (#516)

Extends normalize_generic.py to handle:
- NetCDF grid files (.nc) via xarray — flattens grids to point observations
- XZ-compressed CSV files (.csv.xz) — auto-decompresses before parsing

Used to normalize Ensaio lightning-creek-magnetic (NetCDF, 128K points) and
southern-africa-gravity (compressed CSV, 14K points). Adds xarray and netcdf4
to pipeline requirements.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add pipeline dataset browser to viewer app (#518)

Backend: New /api/pipeline-datasets router that reads normalized Parquet
files from the data pipeline, grids them with scipy, and serves as
GridData JSON compatible with the viewer's deck.gl renderer. Lists all
112 datasets with QA status, noise profiles, and anomaly counts.

Viewer: New /browse route with searchable dataset gallery (filter by
source, sort by size/rows/anomalies). Click any dataset to render it
in the full viewer with heatmap, colorbar, contours, hillshade, and
2D/3D toggle. Direct links supported: /browse/{dataset_id}.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix viewer API proxy: add HTTP/1.1 for Azure Container Apps Envoy (#520)

The viewer nginx was using the default HTTP/1.0 for upstream proxy
connections. Azure Container Apps Envoy rejects HTTP/1.0 with 426
Upgrade Required. The frontend nginx already had proxy_http_version
1.1 but the viewer was missing it.

Also adds pandas/pyarrow to backend deps (needed by pipeline_datasets
router) and makes LoadingScreen error title configurable.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ADLS backend for pipeline datasets in production (#522)

The pipeline datasets router now supports two storage backends:
- local: reads from data/ directory (development)
- adls: reads from Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (production)

Storage account terrascrydata created with geodata container.
Backend container has system-managed identity with Blob Data Reader
role. Config: TERRASCRY_PIPELINE_DATA_STORAGE=adls in cloud mode.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Pre-render pipeline datasets as static GridData JSON (#524)

Instead of downloading Parquet + running scipy gridding on every
request, serve pre-rendered static JSON files. The backend tries
pre-rendered data first, falls back to runtime gridding from Parquet.

New pipeline command: `python -m data.pipeline.cli prerender`
Pre-rendered grids stored at silver/prerendered/grids/ in ADLS.
Raw Parquet data preserved at silver/normalized/magnetics/.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add 6 pre-computed interpretation modes and 3D subsurface objects to pipeline viewer (#526)

Pre-computes FSI, detection probability, dipole match, LoG blobness,
top-hat anomaly, and local entropy grids at prerender time. Viewer
gets a dropdown to switch modes with zero client-side computation.
3D view now uses flat surface with semi-transparent heatmap and
detected anomaly objects placed underground at estimated depths.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add interpretations field to PipelineGridData response model (#528)

Pydantic was stripping the interpretations dict from the pre-rendered
JSON response because the field was missing from the model.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Map extracted noise profiles to GeoSim parameter distributions (closes #533) (#559)

Analyze 103 extracted noise profiles and fit statistical distributions for
each NoiseModel parameter. Produces noise_distributions.json with lognormal/
normal fits, percentiles, sampling ranges, and inter-parameter correlations.
Includes a sampler function for the scenario generator (#530) to consume.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tile-local physics feature extraction module (closes #538) (#561)

Core physics feature engine in geosim/magnetics/physics_features.py with:
- 6-channel V1 schema: analytic signal, tilt, vertical derivative,
  horizontal gradient, local entropy, quality-gated Euler depth
- Pre-padded tile+halo contract (caller provides, module crops)
- Shared gradient computation (dfdx/dfdy/dfdz computed once per tile)
- NaN inpainting before all physics ops, coverage mask returned
- Quality-gated Euler with fallback depth proxy for featureless tiles
- Optional frozen global normalization stats for ML inference
- Full-grid convenience wrapper for backward-compatible prerender path

AI counsel consensus (Codex 0.94 / Gemini 0.95, 3 rounds):
- Transcript: transcripts/20260310_193648_*.md

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tile-based spatial accumulator replacing global O(N×G) IDW gridding (#545) (#563)

Implements TileAccumulator with:
- Fixed-lattice sparse tile keys (TileKey NamedTuple)
- SpatialBinIndex for O(1) insert, O(neighbors) bbox query
- Bounded dirty-tile scheduler with urgency scoring
- Per-tile IDW interpolation with configurable search radius
- Halo-aware tile assembly for derivative context
- get_full_grid() backward compat utility
- 30 tests covering spatial index, IDW, tiling, budget bounding

AI counsel: Codex 0.94 / Gemini 0.98, 3 rounds conference mode.

closes #545

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add SERDP/ESTCP validation metrics for anomaly detection (#544) (#565)

Implements geosim/training/metrics.py with:
- Detection: Pd at fixed Pfa (0.01/0.05/0.10), confusion matrices
- ROC/AUC curves via trapezoidal integration
- Per-class precision/recall/F1 for multi-class classification
- Depth-stratified Pd across configurable depth bins
- Denoising quality: MAE, RMSE, SSIM, PSNR, spectral correlation
- build_validation_report() assembles all metrics into JSON-serializable report
- 27 tests covering all metric types

closes #544

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add randomized scenario generator and U-Net architecture (#530, #539) (#567)

Scenario generator (geosim/training/scenario_generator.py):
- Gaussian copula correlated noise sampling with PSD repair
- Curriculum complexity: 20% empty, 45% simple, 25% medium, 10% dense
- Per-scenario seed triples derived via stable hash
- NoiseDistributionStore consuming noise_distributions.json
- ScenarioWriter, SurveyPlanSampler, DatasetManifestWriter
- 41 tests

U-Net architecture (geosim/training/models/unet.py):
- Asymmetric dual-head residual U-Net: encoder 32/64/128/256
- GroupNorm(8) + SiLU, depthwise-separable residual blocks
- ~870K params (well under 2M budget)
- Noise residual prediction with explicit halo cropping
- Dual heads at 64ch: denoise (64→32→1), classify (64→24→5)
- Dropout2d for future MC dropout uncertainty
- 22 tests (21 pass, 1 skipped for onnxscript)

AI counsel: Codex 0.96/Gemini 0.95 (scenario), Codex 0.93/Gemini 0.95 (unet)

closes #530, closes #539, closes #540

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add physics feature computation on dirty tiles only (closes #546) (#569)

TilePhysicsProcessor wires geosim's extract_features_padded() into the
tile accumulator, computing 6-channel physics features (analytic signal,
tilt derivative, Euler deconvolution, etc.) only for re-gridded tiles.
Per-tile caching with version tracking avoids redundant computation.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI: lint long line in pipeline_datasets.py and add azure deps (#571)

- Break long line at pipeline_datasets.py:307 (E501 > 100 chars)
- Install azure extras in platform-ci.yml so cryptography is available for tests

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add training chip extraction pipeline and physics-informed losses (closes #531, closes #541) (#572)

Chip extractor: grid survey points → extract 64×64/128×128 tiles with
halo, build per-pixel label grids (5 classes), save/load NPZ+JSON pairs.
29 tests.

Physics losses: focal loss (γ=2) with effective-number weighting,
Laplacian physics constraint via FFT (replaces invalid div(B)=0 for
scalar data), combined TerrascryLoss with lambda warmup scheduling.
39 tests.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add data quality validation pipeline for training datasets (closes #535) (#574)

Validates training chips: NaN/Inf detection, label coverage distribution,
noise level consistency with outlier detection, duplicate detection via
content hashing, completeness checks, per-chip denoising quality metrics.

Add data quality validation pipeline (#535) (#575)

The container crashes on startup because SQLite can't open the database
file at ~/.terrascry/datasets/terrascry.db — the parent directory doesn't
exist in the container filesystem. Ensure data_dir is created when
computing the default database URL.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix frontend deploy verify — use az CLI health check instead of curl (#450)

The frontend is behind Cloudflare Access, so curl gets a 403. Use
az containerapp revision list to check the revision health state instead.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add marketing landing page at terrascry.com (#452)

Astro static site at platform/landing/ deployed to Cloudflare Pages.
Dark zinc + emerald design matching the platform design system.
Sections: hero, platform triad (Pathfinder/HIRT/GeoSim), workflow,
use cases (archaeology/UXO/defense/research), ecosystem map, CTA.

DNS updated: terrascry.com now points to CF Pages instead of Azure.
CI workflow deploys on push to main via wrangler.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove hardcoded demo from WelcomeOverlay (#454)

Replace "Load Demo Survey" (hardcoded iron_age_hillfort) with a proper
empty state: "Open Mission File" + "Start Live Survey" as primary
actions, with reference datasets listed below for quick access.

No more "demo" concept in the app — users load data through File menu
or select from available reference datasets.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Show backend connection status honestly (#456)

When the backend is unreachable:
- Toolbar shows red "offline" badge instead of empty version
- WelcomeOverlay shows "Backend unavailable" with clear message
- All backend-dependent actions are hidden until connected
- No more silent failures that lead users on

When connected:
- Toolbar shows actual version from /api/runtime
- WelcomeOverlay shows action buttons and reference datasets

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix frontend deploy verify — use grep instead of exact match (#458)

The az CLI returns health status for all active revisions (one per line).
When multiple revisions are active during rollover, the exact string
match `[[ "$HEALTH" == "Healthy" ]]` fails because the output contains
multiple lines. Use `grep -q` to check that at least one is healthy.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx envsubst destroying proxy config variables (#460)

The default nginx:alpine 20-envsubst-on-templates.sh runs envsubst on
ALL $VAR patterns, replacing nginx variables ($host, $uri, $remote_addr,
$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for, etc.) with empty strings. This broke the
proxy_pass and proxy_set_header directives, causing /api/ requests to
fail with 404.

Fix: use a custom entrypoint that runs envsubst with only $BACKEND_ORIGIN,
preserving all nginx internal variables.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use NGINX_ENVSUBST_FILTER instead of custom entrypoint (#462)

The previous fix (custom entrypoint with CMD override) didn't work in
Azure Container Apps. Use the built-in NGINX_ENVSUBST_FILTER env var
to restrict envsubst to only BACKEND_ORIGIN, preserving nginx variables.
Template goes back into /etc/nginx/templates/ (the standard location).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use $proxy_host to match ACA Envoy Host routing (#465)

$host sends the frontend's domain (app.terrascry.com) as the Host header
to the backend's internal Envoy ingress. ACA Envoy routes by Host header
even on internal traffic, rejecting requests with a 1946-byte 404 before
FastAPI ever sees them. $proxy_host sends the backend's internal FQDN,
which Envoy accepts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix BACKEND_ORIGIN to use HTTPS for ACA internal traffic (#467)

Backend ingress has allowInsecure=false, so ACA Envoy returns 301
redirects for HTTP requests even on internal traffic. Frontend nginx
proxied to http:// and got 301s back — requests never reached FastAPI.

Changed all BACKEND_ORIGIN values from http:// to https:// in the
deploy workflow. ACA provides trusted internal TLS certs between
containers in the same environment.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix nginx proxy — use HTTP + allowInsecure for internal ACA traffic (#469)

The full root cause chain:
1. $host sent wrong Host header → fixed with $proxy_host (PR #465)
2. http:// BACKEND_ORIGIN got 301-redirected by Envoy (allowInsecure=false)
3. https:// BACKEND_ORIGIN failed SSL handshake — backend IP restrictions
   only allow Cloudflare IPs, rejecting internal container traffic

Fix: revert to http:// and set allowInsecure=true on backend ingress.
Internal container-to-container traffic uses HTTP on port 80. External
traffic is secured by Cloudflare IP restrictions + CF Access.

Verified live: /api/runtime, /api/edge/health, /api/reference-datasets
all return 200 OK through the frontend proxy.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 3D terrain lockup — memoize range prop + defer basemap loading

The range prop [rangeMin, rangeMax] created a new array every render,
causing TerrainMesh's expensive canvas compositing useMemo to fire
repeatedly. Memoize it so it only recomputes when values change.

competing with the 2D view for network requests on initial page load.

* Fix origin reference instability causing 3D lockup

useDefaultOrigin called JSON.parse (loadStoredOrigin) on every render,
creating a new object reference each time. This cascaded through
useBasemap3DTexture → tileSpecs recompute → new texture → re-render loop.

Fix: memoize the stored origin read. Also reuse terrainRange for DepthSlice3D.

fix: share AbortSignal across Google Ads API retries to bound total timeout

so rate-limited requests could hang for up to 87s (4 attempts × 20s + backoff).

through all retries. The 20s deadline covers the entire chain including 429
backoff waits — retries still happen but the total time is bounded.

commit.message:
fix: robust column width preservation across all save/load paths

Root cause: three bugs caused column widths to corrupt on save:
1. XML attribute regex assumed fixed order (Id before Target),
   but attribute order in XML is not guaranteed
2. Encrypted saves: xlsx-populate's final outputAsync re-corrupted
   column widths after fixExcelJSColWidths had fixed them
3. Encrypted file reopen: decryptIfNeeded didn't fix column widths,
   so savedOriginalColsXml captured already-corrupted data

Fixes:
- Add buildSheetNameMapping() utility with order-independent XML parsing
- Add restoreColWidthsInXlsxPopulate() to set correct widths in
  xlsx-populate's model before outputAsync (encryption step)
- Add captureColWidthsFromXlsxPopulate() to capture widths from
  xlsx-populate model before outputAsync corrupts them
- Fix decryptIfNeeded to restore column widths after decryption
- Refactor fixExcelJSColWidths to use buildSheetNameMapping

commit.message:
Terraform: Split into app/ and deps/ root modules

Separate the Terraform infrastructure into two independent root modules
to prevent shared singleton resources (Keycloak, Echo) from being
duplicated or destroyed when switching between dev and prod workspaces.

Previously, all resources lived in a single tf/ root module that used
Terraform workspaces for dev/prod separation. This caused a problem:
Keycloak and Echo are cluster-wide singletons (static namespace names,
single instance), but each workspace maintained its own state for them.
Switching to the prod workspace would show plans to create duplicate
Keycloak and Postgres resources, risking data loss or conflicts.

The new layout:

  tf/app/  -- Workspace-multiplexed application resources
    - Caelus API, UI, worker deployments
    - OAuth2-proxy (Helm)
    - Per-environment Postgres, PVCs, RBAC, ingress
    - Uses workspaces: default (dev) and prod
    - Namespaces: caelus/caelus-dev, login/login-dev

  tf/deps/ -- Shared singleton dependencies
    - Keycloak deployment, Postgres, PVC, ingress
    - Echo deployment, service, ingress
    - No workspaces; single state for single instances
    - Namespaces: keycloak, echo

Each project has its own providers.tf, state, and secrets.auto.tfvars.
The keycloak/ and echo/ module directories were moved from tf/ to
tf/deps/. The caelus/ and login/ module directories moved to tf/app/.
Provider paths updated for the new directory depth.

Also updates AGENTS.md with Terraform project documentation and refreshes
all READMEs (tf/README.md, tf/app/README.md, tf/deps/README.md).

commit.message:
fix: distinguish docker daemon down from docker not installed (#16)

* fix: distinguish docker daemon down from docker not installed

The prerequisite check was reporting docker as "not found" when the CLI
was installed but the daemon wasn't running. Now falls back to
`docker --version` to detect the CLI, and reports "daemon not running"
with actionable guidance instead of a misleading "not found" message.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E1tLz3xKmQ5ZsEGC97f2fU

* fix: bind k3d API server to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0

Adds hostIP: "127.0.0.1" to the k3d kubeAPI config. The default
0.0.0.0 binding can be intercepted by network proxies, causing
kubectl to receive 403 Forbidden instead of reaching the API server.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E1tLz3xKmQ5ZsEGC97f2fU

* chore: add .fibers/ to gitignore

The .fibers/ directory contains runtime state (k3d config, cluster
state, policies) generated by `fibers cluster init` and should not
be tracked in version control.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E1tLz3xKmQ5ZsEGC97f2fU

* fix: correctly identify failed step in init pipeline progress

The pipeline was reporting all 4 ClusterManager steps (cluster-create,
api-ready, cilium-install, cilium-ready) as starting simultaneously and
always blaming cluster-create on failure. Now bridges ClusterManager's
progress callbacks so each step reports individually and failures are
attributed to the actual step that failed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E1tLz3xKmQ5ZsEGC97f2fU

* fix: resolve TypeScript cast error in init pipeline test

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E1tLz3xKmQ5ZsEGC97f2fU

* fix: add infra-images step and fix image import for k3d clusters

docker save without --platform produces a multi-arch OCI tar with digest
references not included in the archive, causing "content digest not found"
errors in containerd. This affects both k3d image import (all modes) and
manual ctr import.

Fix: use docker save --platform linux/amd64 to produce single-arch tars,
then docker cp + ctr --address /run/k3s/containerd/containerd.sock import.

Add infra-images pipeline step between api-ready and cilium-install to
pre-import Cilium and pause images before CNI installation. Split
ClusterManager.init() into createCluster() and installCilium() so the
pipeline can insert the import step between them.

Changes:
- images.ts: Replace k3d image import with docker save --platform + ctr
  import pipeline. Add INFRA_IMAGES list and getInfraImages().
- types.ts: Add "infra-images" to InitStepName union.
- init-pipeline.ts: Add step 4 (infra-images) between api-ready and
  cilium-install. Extract bridgeManagerProgress helper. Pipeline is now
  13 steps.
- manager.ts: Split init() into createCluster() + installCilium().
  Remove redundant prerequisite check from manager (pipeline handles it).
- cli/cluster/init.ts: Add "Importing infrastructure images" label.
- state.ts: Add infra-images to ALL_INIT_STEPS.
- All test files updated. 297 tests pass.

Refactor HF _QuantSparseMoe: config-driven token counting, NemotronH detection (#970)

## What does this PR do?

**Type of change:** New feature

**Overview:** Extend `_QuantSparseMoe` to support NemotronH-style MoE
blocks (which use `n_routed_experts` instead of `num_experts`) and
refactor the MoE calibration features to be config-driven and
lazy-initialized.

Key changes:
- `_is_sparse_moe_block` in `plugins/huggingface.py` now accepts
`n_routed_experts` (NemotronH pattern) in addition to `num_experts`
- `_QuantSparseMoe` is refactored: token counting and forced expert
forwarding are now opt-in via config knobs (`moe_calib_experts_ratio`,
`moe_count_expert_calib_tokens`). When both are off (default), forward
is a zero-overhead pass-through.
- Token counting buffer and gate hook are lazy-initialized on first use
instead of eagerly in `_setup`
- `_QuantSparseMoe` gets `layer_sync_moe_local_experts_amax` to sync
input quantizer amax across experts (same as Megatron path)
- Extract shared `sync_moe_experts_input_amax` utility into `utils.py`,
also fixing missing weight amax for experts that received no tokens
during calibration. Megatron's `_MegatronSequentialMLP` now calls this
shared utility.
- `SequentialQuantizer` delegates `amax` property

## Testing

- Updated and added unit tests in `test_sparse_moe.py` covering default
config, lazy init, token counting, top_k restoration, and end-to-end
quantize with both features enabled.
